subject: Anthropology
title: Intellectual Property
description: Learn Intellectual Property in one minute.

hook.text: A corporation patents a plant that Indigenous healers used for centuries. Who owns knowledge? The question is political.

buildup.text: Intellectual property extends ownership to ideas, designs, seeds, and knowledge — things once freely shared.

discovery.text: Biopiracy occurs when corporations patent traditional knowledge without consent or compensation to the source community.

twist.text: IP law favors those who can file patents. Indigenous and local knowledge holders rarely have access to legal systems.

climax.text: Who owns ideas shapes who profits from them. Intellectual property is a battleground between capital and community.

punchline.text: Owning knowledge is the new form of enclosure.

quiz.question: What is biopiracy?
options: Patenting traditional knowledge without consent or compensation
